engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking a PLACE — a concert venue — deciding via the show schedule, prices, ages, address/parking. No expertise sold (not trust), no portfolio-as-proof (not work); browsing the show listings IS the decision.

goal.one_action: Buy tickets to an upcoming show (or, secondarily, join the genre-segmented mailing list)

structure.archetype_note: Venue site: home = dated show grid with per-event ticket redirects; About (history + crew), FAQ (policies), Partners; mailing-list form recurs on multiple pages.

mapped.observed_as: Location & Parking / Venue Policies / Tickets & Payment

unmapped.observed: Shows — dated schedule of 30+ upcoming events, each with date/genre/price/age/lineup and a per-event ticket redirect
unmapped.why_no_fit: The canonical section list has no dated event/showtime schedule primitive; useCases/hero only approximate it

proposed_new.name: event-listings
proposed_new.evidence: Home page is a dated schedule of 30+ shows, each redirecting to a seetickets.com per-event ticketing page — a venue show calendar, not a catalog or booking flow.

components_needed: external ticket button (Buy Tickets / Sold Out states)

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; multi-page structure, CTAs, forms, embeds and off-site ticketing all clearly captured.

gap_findings: No canonical section expresses a dated event/showtime schedule with per-item external ticket redirect — recurring gap for venues/theatres (event-listings).
Genre-preference-segmented newsletter signup (interest checkboxes driving list segmentation) is richer than a plain lead-form but has no dedicated expression.
